message:
feat(codec): add LinesCodec decode_eof, discard-and-recover for oversized lines

Substantially improve LinesCodec robustness with three changes:

1. Discard-and-recover (is_discarding state): when a line exceeds
   max_length, the codec now enters discard mode — it skips bytes until
   the next newline, then resumes normal decoding. This prevents
   unbounded memory retention from a single oversized line and allows
   the stream to continue processing subsequent valid lines.

2. decode_eof: implement proper end-of-stream handling that yields
   trailing data without a newline terminator as a final line, matching
   tokio-util's LinesCodec behavior. Previously this returned an error
   for incomplete lines at EOF.

3. FramedRead test update: framed_read_handles_partial_data now expects
   decode_eof to yield the trailing "partial" line as Ok instead of Err,
   reflecting the corrected decode_eof semantics.

Adds tests for discard-and-recover (oversized then valid) and decode_eof
trailing line emission, plus E2E-CODEC-007 integration test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
